methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $129M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
GITAI, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $129M to date, operates within a highly valued sector. The peer set comprises nine robotics firms at Series B through Series D stages, including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Exotec ($2.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), and Hadrian ($1.6B), alongside a single public comparable. The peer universe spans $300M to $10.0B in valuation.

Two valuation methods are applicable to GITAI. The stage-progression approach applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $129M capital raised, yielding $219M, reflecting typical Series C to next-stage step-up dynamics. The peer-median method anchors to the $1.6B median valuation across nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables. These methods produce materially divergent signals, reflecting both GITAI's earlier capital raise relative to peer valuations and the sector's wide valuation dispersion.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $186M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median point of $910M. The wide range reflects meaningful uncertainty regarding GITAI's relative market position and commercialization trajectory within a capital-intensive, rapidly re-rating sector. Stage-progression conservatism anchors the lower bound; peer comparables establish the upper envelope. Resolution hinges on revenue traction, technology differentiation, and capital efficiency relative to the named peer set.
